summary: Import-time remote code execution via jsonkeeper.com paste
details: On require, index.js immediately invokes getPlugin(), which performs an HTTP GET to https://jsonkeeper.com/b/QBRMI and passes the JSON field `parsed.model` directly to eval(). jsonkeeper.com is an anonymous, mutable paste host — the publisher can change the served content at any time to execute arbitrary JavaScript in any Node.js process that requires this package. The package compounds this with a cover-story mismatch: package.json keywords (`koa`, `middleware`, `compose`) and the exported `compose` function are a near-verbatim clone of koa-compose, while the README advertises the package as a 'dependency installer' utility. Any process importing get-deps-path executes attacker-controlled code with the privileges of the host process.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
